summary: color-style-utils ships postinstall droppers performing system reconnaissance and HTTP exfiltration
details: The package ships two install-time scripts (postinstall.js, postinstall2.js) that combine system data collection with outbound HTTP. postinstall.js loads child_process at line 5 and constructs hostname-bearing payloads at multiple sites (lines 20, 46, 287, 409, 427) before issuing http.request and POST/GET calls (lines 22, 25, 48, 430). postinstall2.js loads child_process at line 21, performs http/https.request at lines 39, 52, and 837, POSTs at line 841, and shells out via execSync('netsh...') at line 652 to interrogate the local network configuration on Windows. The package's stated purpose ("color style utilities") has no legitimate need for child_process, netsh enumeration, hostname collection, or outbound HTTP at install. The structure matches an installer-side reconnaissance and exfiltration dropper firing automatically on `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
